title: Neomarica rupestris
taxonomy: Neomarica rupestris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This genus is further grouped into the subfamily Iridoideae and tribe Irideae. Neomarica rupestris is native to the Americas, specifically in the regions of Brazil and Paraguay. It belongs to the genus Neomarica, which is a group of flowering plants known for their striking and vibrant colors. Neomarica rupestris is also referred to as the 'False Agave' due to its succulent-like appearance, despite not belonging to the Agave family.

intro: Neomarica rupestris, also known as the "Sacred Agouti" or "Venus Flower Basket," is a flowering plant species in the family Iridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neomarica rupestris include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neomarica rupestris is often used to make infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin problems.
